git_commands.description: Initialize a new Git repository in the current directory.
Set the global Git username used for all repositories.
Set the global Git email used for commits.
Set the Git username only for the current repository.
Set the Git email only for the current repository.
Stage all new and modified files for the next commit.
Stage a specific file for the next commit.
Discard all unstaged changes in the working directory.
Discard changes in a specific file.
Show the current state of the working directory and staging area.
Create a commit from staged changes with a message.
Stage all modified tracked files and commit them in one command.
Display the full commit history.
Show commit history from all branches.
Display commit history with a visual graph of branches.
Show commit history in a compact single-line format.
Switch to a specific commit (detached HEAD state).
Create a new branch.
Switch to an existing branch.
Modern command to switch branches.
Merge the specified branch into the current branch.
Delete a local branch after it has been merged.
Add a remote repository to the local project.
Push local commits to a remote repository.
Download a remote repository to your local machine.
Fetch and merge the latest changes from a remote repository.
